findings.description: effectiveDistrictIds is a new array reference every render (no useMemo). When passed to the useEffect dependency array on line 74, it causes the analytics capture('tarot_draw_loaded') to fire on every re-render when districts are active, spamming PostHog with duplicate events. Wrap effectiveDistrictIds computation in useMemo, or change the useEffect dependency to a stable primitive like effectiveDistrictIds?.join(',').

activeDistrictIds is computed as a bare expression (new array every render) and passed as a prop to DistrictPicker (line 109) and used to derive effectiveDistrictIds. Per CLAUDE.md performance standards: 'No inline object/array in render without useMemo'. Should be wrapped in useMemo to avoid unnecessary child re-renders and the cascading effectiveDistrictIds instability.

Duplicate test file. There are now TWO test files for DistrictChips: components/explore/district-chips.test.tsx (new, 107 lines) AND components/explore/__tests__/district-chips.test.tsx (existing, updated). Both test the same component with overlapping coverage. The new file should be removed, or the tests should be consolidated into one location.

districts[0] uses unsafe array indexing. CLAUDE.md states 'Never use unsafe [0] array indexing. Use the project's first() helper function instead.' However, this is guarded by the preceding condition (!gpsAvailable && districts[0]), so it is safe at runtime. Flagging for CLAUDE.md compliance, not a bug.
Docstring for _query_district_shops still says 'Query shops within a district' (singular). Should read 'Query shops within one or more districts' to reflect the multi-district signature change.

handleTryDifferentDistrict sets selectedDistrictIds to [] which in near-me mode triggers GPS-based fetch and in no-GPS mode falls back to districts[0]. The behavior is correct but the function name 'TryDifferentDistrict' is now misleading -- it clears all districts rather than trying a different one. Consider renaming to handleClearDistricts or handleResetFilter for clarity.

skipped.reason: selectedDistrictIds.includes() is O(n) per district button render. Considered flagging as performance issue per CLAUDE.md 'Map over Array.find() in list renders', but district count is inherently small (10-20 max for Taiwan cities), so a Set pre-index would be over-engineering.

district_ids param is appended via string concatenation rather than URLSearchParams.set(). Considered flagging as inconsistent with the lat/lng params above it, but the values are pre-sorted UUIDs with no special characters, so URL encoding is not needed.

Duplicate parsing pattern (comma-split list comprehension) appears in both tarot_draw and vibe_shops. Considered flagging as DRY violation, but it is a 1-line idiom and extracting a helper would add indirection for minimal benefit.
